title: Safe Wild Foraging & Botany Basics
subject: Life Science & Botany
description: Learn to identify wild edible plants, avoid toxic lookalikes, and apply Universal Edibility Principles safely.
learningObjectives: Identify common safe wild plants (Dandelions, Wild Pine Needle Tea).
Recognize toxic indicators ('Leaves of three', milky sap, umbrella white berries).
Understand plant anatomy (leaves, stem, roots, flower).

lessons.title: Safe vs Toxic Plant Characteristics
lessons.content: Rule #1 of wild foraging: Never ingest any plant or mushroom unless you have 100% positive identification! Dandelions are 100% edible (leaves, flowers, and roots). Poison Ivy contains urushiol oil causing severe allergic rashes — remember: 'Leaves of three, let it be!'

lessons.title: Pine Needle Tea & Vitamin C
lessons.content: Eastern White Pine needles contain high concentrations of Vitamin C (up to 4-5 times higher than lemons!). Steep green needles in hot water for 10 minutes to make a survival tonic.

flashcards.definition: The toxic skin irritant chemical found in Poison Ivy, Oak, and Sumac.
A 100% edible wild plant with yellow flowers, serrated leaves, and high nutritional value.
Matching 100% of physical features against an official field guide before harvesting.

questions.question: What is the common rhyme used to remember and avoid Poison Ivy?
Which wild plant has 100% edible leaves, flowers, and roots?

practicalChallenge.title: Wild Leaf Identification Challenge
practicalChallenge.instruction: Find 3 different plant leaves outdoors. Examine the leaf margins (smooth vs serrated) and sketch them in your field journal!
